paper_title: Study on effectiveness of activated charcoal and wood vinegar on prevention of piglet diarrhea

entities.description: Lead author and corresponding author of the study.
Co-author from Tokyo University of Agriculture and Technology.

entities.id: College of Agriculture, Can Tho University
entities.description: Educational and research institution in Vietnam.

entities.id: Faculty of Agriculture, Tokyo University of Agriculture and Technology
entities.description: Educational and research institution in Japan.
The branch of medicine that deals with the prevention, control, diagnosis, and treatment of disease, disorder, and injury in animals.
A common digestive disease in suckling and post-weaning pigs causing economic losses.
The ability of microorganisms like E. coli to resist the effects of an antibiotic.
A material used for absorption of harmful bacteria and toxins in the digestive tract.
An organic acidifier used for digestive sterilization and stimulating useful microbes.
Statistical software used for data analysis.

entities.id: Statistical Analysis (Chi-square and General Linear Model)
entities.description: Mathematical methods used to analyze experimental data.
Digital records and observations from 217 suckling piglets and 226 post-weaning pigs.

entities.id: Average ratio of diarrhea in suckling pigs (7.37%)
entities.description: Measurement of diarrhea frequency in the experimental suckling group.

entities.id: Average ratio of diarrhea in post weaning pigs (1.32%)
entities.description: Measurement of diarrhea frequency in the experimental post-weaning group.
Efficiency measure of how well the piglets convert feed into body mass.
Average body weight reached by piglets in the experimental group.
Percentage growth in farmer profit compared to the control group.
